evals.prompt: I'm building a RAG pipeline and need to choose a vector DB. Can you benchmark FAISS vs Qdrant on the SQuAD dataset using 200 docs? I'm running Ollama locally with nomic-embed-text pulled. I care most about query speed.
evals.expected_output: Runs the benchmark with --dataset squad --indexers faiss,qdrant --max-docs 200 --model ollama/nomic-embed-text, shows results table, and recommends the faster indexer based on the query latency numbers.
assertions: Runs embenx benchmark with squad dataset
Limits to faiss and qdrant indexers
Uses max-docs around 200 (or less for quick test)
Uses ollama/nomic-embed-text or similar local model
Provides a recommendation based on query latency

evals.prompt: I have a local file at ./data/articles.jsonl where the text is in a column called 'body'. I want to compare all available vector indexers to find the one with the smallest memory footprint. Start with 100 docs.
evals.expected_output: Runs benchmark with --dataset json --data-files ./data/articles.jsonl --text-column body --max-docs 100, checks if the file exists first, and recommends the indexer with lowest memory overhead.
assertions: Uses --dataset json flag for local file
Mentions checking if the file exists or handles the missing file gracefully
Frames the recommendation around memory footprint

evals.prompt: Which vector database is best for a small local semantic search project? I have about 50k documents and I don't want to pay for cloud embeddings. I also want to keep the index files around after benchmarking so I can inspect them.
evals.expected_output: Suggests a benchmarking plan using a local Ollama model (no cloud cost), passes --no-cleanup to preserve index files, and recommends an indexer suitable for local use with good self-hosting characteristics.
assertions: Recommends using a local/Ollama embedding model to avoid cloud costs
Uses or mentions the --no-cleanup flag to preserve index files
Recommends starting with a smaller max-docs for the test run before scaling to 50k
Gives a concrete recommendation for local self-hosted use (e.g., Chroma or LanceDB)
